methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $185M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $274M - $1.3B.

data.narrative:
Heart Aerospace, a Series B space and aerospace company that has raised $185M to date, is evaluated against a universe of 3 same-stage private peers (Aalyria, Hubble Network, Sift) and 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $185M raised proxy, implying a $352M valuation. Peer-median anchoring across the 3 Series B space and aerospace comparables yields a $400M median, though with material dispersion: the peer range spans $274M to $1.3B, reflecting wide performance variance in the sector. Public-comp P/S anchoring cannot be applied because target revenue is not disclosed. A third method—public-comp benchmarking against the 5 space and aerospace tickers—would require revenue, which is unavailable for private companies.

The triangulation of the two applicable methods supports a valuation range of $299M (low) to $460M (high), with a median of $376M. Key uncertainties center on the wide dispersion within the peer set, particularly the $1.3B outlier (Aalyria), and the inability to apply public-market multiples without revenue disclosure. The range reflects defensible but constrained methodology, relying entirely on stage-progression heuristics and a small private-peer cohort with heterogeneous fundamentals.
